data.text: I’ve confirmed the repo currently has only the internal doctor tests, and the command-level coverage likely lives in another `cmd` test file. Next I’m reading the implementation and nearby command tests to trace how findings are rendered and fixed.

I’ve got the current shape: the doctor only scans oversized fields, and the command just formats that report plus `--dangling`. I’m now tracing how parent chains and deps are represented so the new pass can stay deterministic and only remove the back-edge targets under `--fix`.

package cmd

import (
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadCreateRejectsDependsOnParentAncestor(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	rootOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Root", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	rootID := strings.TrimSpace(rootOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, rootID)

	parentOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Parent", "--type", "task", "--parent", rootID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	parentID := strings.TrimSpace(parentOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, parentID)

	t.Run("direct-parent", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child direct", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", parentID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})

	t.Run("grandparent-ancestor", func(t *testing.T) {
		_, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Child ancestor", "--type", "task", "--parent", parentID, "--depends-on", rootID)
		require.Error(t, err)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "ancestor in the parent chain")
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), rootID)
		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), parentID+" -> "+rootID)
	})
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newBeadTestRoot(t *testing.T, workingDir string) *CommandFactory {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	t.Setenv("DDX_BEAD_DIR", ".ddx")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxroot.InTree(workingDir), 0o755))
	return NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
}

func TestBeadCommandsCRUDLifecycle(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Fix auth bug", "--type", "bug", "--priority", "1", "--labels", "backend,urgent", "--acceptance", "bug is fixed")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	createdID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, createdID)
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(workingDir, ddxroot.DirName, "beads.jsonl"))

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var created map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &created))
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, created["id"])
	assert.Equal(t, "Fix auth bug", created["title"])
	assert.Equal(t, "bug", created["issue_type"])
	assert.Equal(t, "open", created["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), created["priority"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", createdID, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me", "--labels", "backend")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	updatedOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", createdID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var updated map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(updatedOut), &updated))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", updated["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", updated["owner"])
	require.Len(t, updated["labels"], 1)

	listOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "list", "--status", "in_progress", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var listed []map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(listOut), &listed))
	require.Len(t, listed, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, createdID, listed[0]["id"])

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "close", createdID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	statusOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "status", "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var status map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(statusOut), &status))
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["total"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(1), status["closed"])
	assert.Equal(t, float64(0), status["open"])
}

func TestBeadUpdateStatusUsesLifecycleTransition(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Transition target", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "blocked")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "transition_requires_external_blocker")

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--status", "in_progress", "--assignee", "me")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var got map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &got))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", got["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "me", got["owner"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimUsesExplicitAssignee(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim", "--assignee", "alice")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	showOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "show", id, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var bead map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(showOut), &bead))
	assert.Equal(t, "in_progress", bead["status"])
	assert.Equal(t, "alice", bead["owner"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-at"])
	assert.NotEmpty(t, bead["claimed-pid"])
}

func TestBeadCommandsClaimFallsBackToCallerIdentity(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	t.Setenv("USER", "runtime-agent")

	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "Claim me too", "--type", "task")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	id := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)

	_, err = executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "update", id, "--claim")

data.text: The first patch missed the exact file context, so I’m re-reading the current doctor implementation and then I’ll patch it in smaller slices to avoid clobbering unrelated code.

1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"encoding/json"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"path/filepath"
     7	
     8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
     9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
    10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
    11		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    12	)
    13	
    14	// newBeadDoctorCommand wires `ddx bead doctor` / `ddx bead doctor --fix`.
    15	//
    16	// Scan mode (no flags): exits non-zero if any field on any bead exceeds the
    17	// per-field cap (ddx-f8a11202), reporting the offending bead id, field, and
    18	// size. Safe to run on any tree — no mutations.
    19	//
    20	// Fix mode (--fix): rewrites oversized fields in place. Before touching the
    21	// tracker the command writes a timestamped backup under .ddx/backups/ so
    22	// the original file is always recoverable. Overflow content persists as
    23	// artifacts under .ddx/executions/<bead-id>/repair-<timestamp>/ and a
    24	// repair event is appended to each rewritten bead. Idempotent — the second
    25	// invocation exits 0 without writing because the scan is clean.
    26	//
    27	// Dangling-success mode (--dangling): detects in_progress beads whose last
    28	// execution produced task_succeeded but CloseWithEvidence never ran. Safe
    29	// to run on any tree — no mutations unless --fix is also passed.
    30	// (ddx-2b2d114e)
    31	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadDoctorCommand() *cobra.Command {
    32		cmd := &cobra.Command{
    33			Use:   "doctor",
    34			Short: "Detect (and optionally repair) beads.jsonl rows with oversized fields",
    35			Long: `Scan the bead tracker for fields that exceed the per-field size cap.
    36	
    37	Bead fields (description, acceptance, notes, events[].body, events[].summary)
    38	are capped at 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip cleanly through
    39	bd import (upstream's Dolt TEXT column limit). Fields over the cap usually
    40	come from a writer bug that landed before the cap was enforced — for
    41	example a reviewer stream dumped verbatim into an event body.
    42	
    43	Without --fix this command only reports offending rows and exits non-zero.
    44	With --fix it:
    45	
    46	  1. Writes a timestamped backup to .ddx/backups/ before any mutation.
    47	  2. Truncates each oversized field to the cap using head+tail+marker.
    48	  3. Writes the full original payload to
    49	     .ddx/executions/<bead>/repair-<timestamp>/<field>.log so forensics
    50	     remain possible.
    51	  4. Appends a kind=repair event to every rewritten bead.
    52	
    53	Idempotent: once a tracker is clean, running --fix again is a no-op.
    54	
    55	With --dangling: scan for in_progress beads whose last execution produced
    56	task_succeeded but whose bead-close step never ran (dangling-success).
    57	For each finding, reports whether result_rev is reachable from HEAD:
    58	  - reachable=true:  merge succeeded, close didn't. Safe to close manually
    59	    with: ddx bead close <bead-id>
    60	  - reachable=false: the merge itself was never applied (dangling commit).
    61	    The result_rev is shown for manual git cherry-pick or re-attempt.
    62	(ddx-2b2d114e)`,
    63			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    64				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
    65				if workspace == "" {
    66					return fmt.Errorf("bead doctor: no .ddx workspace found")
    67				}
    68				path := ddxroot.JoinProject(workspace, "beads.jsonl")
    69	
    70				doFix, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("fix")
    71				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
    72				doDangling, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("dangling")
    73	
    74				// --dangling mode: detect dangling-success beads (ddx-2b2d114e).
    75				if doDangling {
    76					findings, err := agent.DetectDanglingSuccessBeads(workspace)
    77					if err != nil {
    78						return fmt.Errorf("bead doctor --dangling: %w", err)
    79					}
    80					if asJSON {
    81						enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
    82						enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
    83						return enc.Encode(struct {
    84							Clean    bool                           `json:"clean"`
    85							Findings []agent.DanglingSuccessFinding `json:"findings"`
    86						}{
    87							Clean:    len(findings) == 0,
    88							Findings: findings,
    89						})
    90					}
    91					out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
    92					if len(findings) == 0 {
    93						fmt.Fprintf(out, "bead doctor --dangling: clean (no dangling-success beads detected)\n")
    94						return nil
    95					}
    96					fmt.Fprintf(out, "bead doctor --dangling: %d finding(s):\n", len(findings))
    97					for _, fi := range findings {
    98						reachableStr := "reachable=false (dangling commit — merge never applied)"
    99						recovery := "  recovery: git cherry-pick or re-run ddx try " + fi.BeadID
   100						if fi.Reachable {
   101							reachableStr = "reachable=true (merge succeeded, close didn't run)"
   102							recovery = "  recovery: ddx bead close " + fi.BeadID + "  # or re-run ddx try"
   103						}
   104						fmt.Fprintf(out, "  %s  attempt=%s  result_rev=%s\n    %s\n%s\n",
   105							fi.BeadID, fi.AttemptID, fi.ResultRev, reachableStr, recovery)
   106					}
   107					return fmt.Errorf("bead doctor --dangling: %d finding(s) detected", len(findings))
   108				}
   109	
   110				var report bead.DoctorReport
   111				var err error
   112				if doFix {
   113					report, err = bead.BeadDoctorFix(path, nil)
   114				} else {
   115					report, err = bead.BeadDoctor(path)
   116				}
   117				if err != nil {
   118					return err
   119				}
   120	
   121				if asJSON {
   122					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
   123					enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
   124					return enc.Encode(struct {
   125						Path     string               `json:"path"`
   126						Fixed    bool                 `json:"fixed"`
   127						Clean    bool                 `json:"clean"`
   128						Findings []bead.DoctorFinding `json:"findings"`
   129					}{
   130						Path:     report.Path,
   131						Fixed:    doFix && !report.Clean(),
   132						Clean:    report.Clean(),
   133						Findings: report.Findings,
   134					})
   135				}
   136	
   137				out := cmd.OutOrStdout()
   138				if report.Clean() {
   139					fmt.Fprintf(out, "bead doctor: %s — clean (no fields exceed %d bytes)\n", path, bead.MaxFieldBytes)
   140					return nil
   141				}
   142				fmt.Fprintf(out, "bead doctor: %s — %d finding(s) exceeding %d-byte cap:\n", path, len(report.Findings), bead.MaxFieldBytes)
   143				for _, f := range report.Findings {
   144					fmt.Fprintf(out, "  %s  %s  %d bytes  head=%q\n", f.BeadID, f.FieldPath, f.SizeBytes, f.SampleHead)
   145				}
   146				if doFix {
   147					fmt.Fprintf(out, "\nrepair complete. backup written to %s/backups/. artifact sidecars under %s/executions/<bead>/repair-*/\n", filepath.Dir(path), filepath.Dir(path))
   148					return nil
   149				}
   150				// Non-fix scan: non-zero exit via cobra error so CI can catch it.
   151				return fmt.Errorf("bead doctor: %d finding(s) — run `ddx bead doctor --fix` to repair", len(report.Findings))
   152			},
   153		}
   154		cmd.Flags().Bool("fix", false, "Rewrite oversized fields in place after writing a backup")
   155		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output findings as JSON")
   156		cmd.Flags().Bool("dangling", false, "Detect in_progress beads with a prior task_succeeded result (dangling-success, ddx-2b2d114e)")
   157		return cmd
   158	}

data.text: The core implementation is in place. I’m adding a regression that exercises the real scan/fix path with one safe dependency plus two back-edges, so the test proves both detection and selective repair instead of just “it didn’t crash.”

I’m adding the command-level regression now. It will hit the CLI in JSON mode so the test can assert the stable `code` and `field_path` fields directly, then verify the rewritten file is clean afterward.

I’ve finished the code and test edits. Next I’m formatting the touched files and running the focused tests first so any compile or behavioral issues show up before the broader package and pre-commit gates.

data.text: The first run exposed a real bug, not a test issue: dependency removals were applied one by one, so later indices shifted and one back-edge survived. I’m switching that repair path to a grouped filter so all offending edges are removed from the original array state in one pass.

The bug was in the repair algorithm, not the detector: removing array entries by index one-at-a-time is unsafe once the first removal shifts the second. I’ve switched that to a grouped filter and I’m reformatting before rerunning the focused tests.
